response: Research problem: What single access-friction level should a digital service set to maximize platform payoff from benign participation net of malicious harm when user type is private and users choose strategically whether to participate? A common friction level changes participation incentives differently across latent benign and malicious types, creating a deterrence-versus-participation trade-off. The policy cannot condition on a user's true type or assume that malicious users are identified before access. Differences in friction sensitivity are part of the modeled type-dependent payoff structure, not an observed classification fact. Nor does the model establish that increasing friction always improves safety; its net effect depends on both types' participation responses and the modeled payoffs.
